title: phlomis brachyodon subsp. brachyodon
intro: Phlomis brachyodon subsp. brachyodon is a subspecies of the Phlomis brachyodon plant, a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, also known as Jerusalem sage. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Phlomis brachyodon subsp. brachyodon include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic to treat wounds and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Phlomis brachyodon subsp. brachyodon include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or extracting the plant's active compounds using solvents like ethanol or glycerin.
